perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT may invent new, original, and ornamental or useful inventions in the course of or related to RECIPIENT’S business relationship with OWNER ("the Inventions").
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT shall not invent new, original, and ornamental or useful inventions in the course of or related to RECIPIENT’S business relationship with OWNER ("the Inventions").
perturbation.explanation: The modified text forbids the RECIPIENT from inventing new things during their business relationship, directly contradicting the initial statement that they 'may' invent. This introduces an in-text contradiction, because it states that RECIPIENT shall not invent. This change would affect the Intellectual Property section of the contract, specifically patent assignment because it is not clear if inventions should be assigned or not, since RECIPIENT is barred from inventing in the first place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECIPIENT hereby assigns and/or transfers to OWNER, its successors or assigns, the entire right, title, and interest in and to said Inventions, and any patent and patent applications deriving there from for any such invention in the United States and 3throughout the world, including the right to file foreign applications directly in the name of OWNER and to claim for any such foreign applications any priority rights to which such applications are entitled under international conventions, treaties, or otherwise; and to cooperate with OWNER as may be necessary or desirable for obtaining, sustaining, reissuing, or enforcing said patent or patent applications in the United States and throughout the world for said Inventions, and for perfecting, recording, or maintaining any such title in OWNER.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the RECIPIENT 'may invent' inventions. The changed text explicitly states that the RECIPIENT 'shall not invent' inventions. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assigns the rights to 'said Inventions' to the OWNER, because there would be no inventions to assign if the RECIPIENT is barred from inventing.

perturbation.original_text: OWNER agrees to disclose INFORMATION to RE CIPIENT to facilitate possible future business dealings between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: OWNER agrees to disclose INFORMATION to RECIPIENT to facilitate possible future business dealings between the parties; however, OWNER reserves the right to withhold any information at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text adds that the OWNER can withhold any information they want. This directly contradicts the earlier statement that the OWNER agrees to disclose information. This creates an in-text contradiction and affects the entire agreement because, if the OWNER can decide what to share, the purpose of the NDA and the restrictions on the RECIPIENT become uncertain. RECIPIENT might not get all the required information, hindering business dealings, yet still, be bound by the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECIPIENT agrees to receive such INFORMAT ION and to refrain from copying, disclosing, using, selling, or offering for sale any and all of said INFORMATION, other than at the request of OWNER, with the exceptions as provided in paragraph C herein. RECIPIENT agrees to keep confidential and refrain from disclosing any an d all of the INFORMATION, and to take all necessary and reasonable steps to prevent unauthorized disclosure or use of any and all of the INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives the OWNER the right to withhold any information at its sole discretion. The contradicted text requires the RECIPIENT to safeguard and refrain from disclosing 'any and all' of the INFORMATION. If the OWNER can withhold information, it creates a contradiction as to what information the RECIPIENT is actually responsible for protecting, impacting the entire agreement by making it unclear what the RECIPIENT is bound to.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT shall not institute any action or suit at law or in equity against OWNER, nor institute, prosecute or in any way aid in the institution or prosecution of any claim, demand, action, or cause of action arising out of the INFORMATION or any INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY thereof, including but not limited to, claim, demand, action, or cause of action for in validating any INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY of OWNER.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT shall not institute any action or suit at law or in equity against OWNER unless such action arises from gross negligence or willful misconduct on the part of OWNER, nor institute, prosecute or in any way aid in the institution or prosecution of any claim, demand, action, or cause of action arising out of the INFORMATION or any INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY thereof, including but not limited to, claim, demand, action, or cause of action for invalidating any INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY of OWNER.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text allows the RECIPIENT to sue the OWNER in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct, which directly contradicts the original text's prohibition of any legal action against the OWNER. This creates an in-text contradiction. The original covenant not to sue is compromised, introducing ambiguity and potential legal challenges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECIPIENT agrees that should RECIPIENT breach a ny of the promises contained in this Agreement that OWNER would suffer irreparable harm and OWNER w ould be without adequate remedy at law and that OWNER may obtain injunctive relief, including specific performance of the Agreement, as well as monetary award for damages suffered by OWNER for RECIPIENT’S breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits the RECIPIENT from instituting any legal action against the OWNER. The changed text now allows such actions in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the OWNER would be without adequate remedy at law should RECIPIENT breach any promises in the Agreement. The changed text now allows the RECIPIENT to sue OWNER in certain circumstances, effectively changing the OWNER's initial promise. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, construed, and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of California in the Central District of California.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, construed, and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text specifies that the agreement will follow the laws of Delaware instead of California, creating a direct conflict. This introduces an in-text contradiction, because it changes which State laws are followed. This also affects the jurisdiction mentioned later in the paragraph, as it's tied to the location where California laws are applied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, construed, and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. Any dispute involving the terms or conditions of this Agreement shall be brought in the Central District of California or a California State court of competent subject matter jurisdiction therein. Each of the parties he reby submits to the personal jurisdiction of said court.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies California law and the Central District of California. The changed text replaces California law with Delaware law. The contradicted text states that disputes will be brought in the Central District of California but now governed by Delaware law. This creates a direct conflict because the jurisdiction (California) is linked to laws (Delaware) from a different state. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction as to which state's laws and jurisdiction should be followed.
